All are welcome to the blessings of salvation, to those for whom those blessings are intended. In Christ there is enough for everyone, and enough for each person. Those who are satisfied with the world and see no need for Christ do not thirst. They have no anxiety about their souls; but where God gives grace, he gives a desire for it; and where he has given a desire for it, he will satisfy it. Come to Christ, for he is the fountain opened, he is the rock from which water flows. Come to holy practices, to the streams that bring joy to the city of our God. Come to the healing waters, come to the living waters. Our Savior referred to this. Come and buy; make it your own by applying the grace of the gospel to yourselves. Come and eat; enjoy it even more. The world fails to meet our expectations; we promise ourselves water, at least, and are disappointed; but Christ surpasses our expectations. We come to him, and we find wine and milk. The gifts offered to us are beyond price. They have already been paid for; for Christ purchased them with the full price of his own blood. Our needs are countless, and we have nothing to supply them; if Christ and heaven are ours, we see ourselves forever indebted to free grace. Pay close attention; let proud hearts humble themselves; not only come, but accept God’s offers. All the wealth and pleasure in the world will not bring lasting comfort and contentment to the soul. They do not even satisfy the body's appetites; for all is vanity and frustration. Let the disappointments we experience in the world help us turn to Christ and seek satisfaction in him alone. Then, and not before, we will find rest for our souls. Listen, and your soul will live. How easily happiness is offered to us! By the sure mercies of David, we understand the Messiah. All his mercies are covenant mercies; they were purchased by him, promised through him, and dispensed to us from his hand. We do not know how to find the way to the waters, but Christ has been given as a leader to show us what to do and enable us to do it. Our responsibility is to obey him and follow him. And there is no coming to the Father except through him. He is the Holy One of Israel, true to his promises; and he has promised to glorify Christ by giving him the Gentiles for an inheritance.

Here is a gracious offer of pardon, peace, and all happiness. It will not be in vain to seek God now that his word calls to us and his Spirit strives with us. But there will come a day when he cannot be found. Such a time may come in this life; it is certain that at death and judgment the door will be shut. There must be both a change of direction and a change of mind. We must alter our judgments about people and things. It is not enough to stop evil practices; we must strive against evil thoughts. To repent is to return to our Lord, against whom we have rebelled. If we do so, God will multiply his pardon as we have multiplied our offenses. But let no one trifle with this abundant mercy or use it as an opportunity to sin. People’s thoughts about sin, Christ, and holiness, about this world and the next, differ greatly from God's; but they differ most in the matter of forgiveness. We forgive and cannot forget; but when God forgives sin, he remembers it no more. The power of his word in the realms of providence and grace is as certain as it is in nature. Sacred truth produces a spiritual change in people’s minds that neither rain nor snow can produce on earth. It will not return to the Lord without producing significant effects. If we take a special view of the church, we will see what great things God has done and will do for it. The Jews will return to their own land; this will represent the blessings promised. Gospel grace will bring about great changes in people. Delivered from God’s wrath, the converted sinner finds peace in his conscience; and love compels him to devote himself to the service of his Redeemer. Instead of being irreverent, quarrelsome, selfish, or sensual, he is patient, humble, kind, and peaceful. The hope of helping in such a work should urge us to spread the gospel of salvation. And you, Spirit of all truth, help us to have such views of the fullness, freeness, and greatness of the rich mercy in Christ as will remove from us all narrow views of sovereign grace.
